It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rhaponticum carthamoides include inulin, glycosides, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rhaponticum carthamoides include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, as well as capsules and tablets containing standardized extracts of its bioactive compounds.

uses: Rapuntium thermale, also known as meadow senna, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
